name: Compliance Assessment
description: Universal compliance assessment engine. Produces an exhaustive compliance matrix with verdicts for every requirement, evidence citations back to source documents, and coverage statistics. Framework-agnostic: works with any regulatory MCP (ISO 27001, DORA, NIS2, etc.), custom requirement documents, or direct input.
estimated_duration: 30-120 min depending on framework scope and document volume

mcp_tools.guidance: Index all uploaded documents in Phase 1. In Phase 3, search with multiple query variations per requirement (synonyms, abbreviations, related terms). Use get_surrounding when a match needs context from adjacent sections. Always retrieve verbatim quotes for evidence.

Use list_security_frameworks to confirm available frameworks. Use get_framework_controls to enumerate complete control lists for ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIST CSF, etc. Use get_security_control for individual control details when building matrix rows.
Use list_eu_regulations during scoping to identify applicable EU regulations. Use get_eu_article to enumerate requirements during Phase 2. Record regulation version from get_eu_article responses.
Use for ISO 21434 and UNECE R155 assessments. auto_search_requirements returns full requirement lists by section. Use check_r155_compliance for UNECE R155 specific checks.

Store completed domain groups to disk after Phase 3 processing (key format: group_{name}). In Phase 4, store coverage_stats, scope_and_methodology, org_profile, documents, and applicable_frameworks. Then call wkfl_export_report to assemble the final deliverable report. Use list_states for session resume.

intake_question_templates._description: Framework-keyed curated intake questions. Each question is load-bearing: the answer directly affects N/A exclusions or verdict determination. The agent should use these as a starting set and add context-specific questions based on document content.

ISO_27001.question: Do you have an existing Statement of Applicability (SoA)? If so, which Annex A controls are marked as not applicable?
ISO_27001.reason: SoA exclusions directly map to not_applicable verdicts, preventing unnecessary evidence searches
ISO_27001.impact: Controls listed as excluded will be marked not_applicable with client attestation

ISO_27001.question: Does your organization develop software in-house or outsource development?
ISO_27001.reason: Determines applicability of A.8.25–A.8.33 (secure development lifecycle controls)
ISO_27001.impact: If no software development, ~9 controls become not_applicable

ISO_27001.question: Does your organization have physical premises, data centres, or server rooms that require physical access controls?
ISO_27001.impact: Fully cloud-hosted orgs with no physical infrastructure may exclude physical controls

ISO_27001.question: Does your organization process personally identifiable information (PII) or act as a PII processor for clients?
ISO_27001.reason: Determines applicability of A.5.34 (privacy and PII protection) and related controls
ISO_27001.impact: Orgs that process no PII may mark A.5.34 as not_applicable

ISO_27001.question: Does your organization use cryptographic controls (encryption, digital signatures, key management)?
ISO_27001.reason: Determines applicability of A.8.24 (cryptography) and depth of evidence required
ISO_27001.impact: Affects verdict for A.8.24 and may affect A.5.31 (legal/regulatory requirements)

ISO_27001.question: Does your organization use third-party suppliers or cloud services that process, store, or transmit information on your behalf?
ISO_27001.reason: Determines applicability of A.5.19–A.5.23 (supplier relationship controls)
ISO_27001.impact: Orgs with no third-party suppliers may have reduced scope for supplier controls

ISO_27001.question: Has a formal ISMS scope been defined and documented (clause 4.3)? If yes, what boundaries and applicability does it state?
ISO_27001.reason: Clause 4.3 is a mandatory certification requirement. Without a defined scope, the entire ISMS is unbounded and no control verdicts are meaningful.
ISO_27001.impact: Missing scope is the single most common certification blocker. Surfaces it before the Annex A pass.

ISO_27001.question: Has a formal information security risk assessment been conducted (clause 6.1.2)? Is there a documented risk treatment plan?
ISO_27001.reason: Clause 6.1.2 requires a risk assessment process with defined criteria. The risk treatment plan (6.1.3) determines which Annex A controls are selected and why.
ISO_27001.impact: Without a risk assessment, Annex A control selection has no justification. This is a mandatory clause — not auditor-discretionary.

ISO_27001.question: Has the organization conducted internal audits of the ISMS (clause 9.2)? Is there a management review process (clause 9.3)?
ISO_27001.reason: Clauses 9.2 and 9.3 are mandatory. Organizations pursuing initial certification almost never have these, but the gap must be surfaced explicitly.
ISO_27001.impact: Missing internal audit and management review are certification-blocking findings that cannot be discovered during Annex A control mapping.

ISO_27001.question: Has top management established an information security policy (clause 5.2) and assigned ISMS roles and responsibilities (clause 5.3)?
ISO_27001.reason: Clauses 5.1–5.3 require demonstrated leadership commitment, a policy with objectives, and assigned authorities. These are distinct from Annex A policy controls.
ISO_27001.impact: Clause 5 non-compliance indicates fundamental ISMS governance gaps that affect every downstream control.

prompting_guidance:
Drive through 4 phases sequentially. Use check_stage_completeness with phase_id at each gate.

**Phase 1 — Scoping & Document Intake:**
Ask the user what they want assessed and what documents they have. Index all documents via document-index MCP. Build org_profile from document content + user input. Identify applicable frameworks. Generate ONLY load-bearing intake questions — if the agent would reach the same verdict regardless of the answer, don't ask. Batch questions in a single round. Record responses.

For ISO 27001: intake MUST cover clauses 4–10 (management system requirements) BEFORE Annex A. Clauses 4.3 (scope), 5.2 (policy), 6.1.2 (risk assessment), 6.1.3 (risk treatment plan), 9.2 (internal audit), and 9.3 (management review) are mandatory certification requirements. Missing any of these is a certification blocker that cannot be discovered during Annex A control mapping. Ask about them explicitly in intake — do not defer to Phase 3.

**Phase 2 — Requirement Enumeration:**
For each applicable framework, call the relevant regulatory MCP to get the COMPLETE control/requirement list. Do not sample or abbreviate. Apply N/A exclusions from intake responses. Build the full requirement_register with stable IDs. Group requirements into domain slices (~15-20 each).

For ISO 27001: enumerate clauses 4–10 as the FIRST domain group before any Annex A controls. These are not optional — they are the management system backbone. An organization can have every Annex A control implemented and still fail certification if clauses 4–10 are missing.

**Phase 3 — Evidence Assessment:**
Work through domain_groups sequentially. For each requirement: search document-index with multiple query variations, retrieve full sections + surrounding context, extract verbatim quotes, determine verdict (compliant/partial/not_found/contradicted/not_applicable), assign confidence. After completing each domain group, store it via wkfl_store_state and drop from working memory. Only carry current group in context.

**Phase 4 — Coverage Validation & Report Delivery:**
Load all completed groups via wkfl_load_state. Verify every applicable requirement has a matrix row. Calculate coverage statistics. Surface ambiguous findings for user review. Generate scope_and_methodology section. Store all final state keys (org_profile, documents, applicable_frameworks, coverage_stats, scope_and_methodology) via wkfl_store_state. Call wkfl_export_report to assemble the formatted report document. Present the full report to the user as the assessment deliverable. Set report_ready = true.

**Question Discipline:** If the agent would reach the same verdict regardless of the answer, don't ask. Batch questions. Two touchpoints: intake questions (Phase 1) and ambiguous findings review (Phase 4). Do not interrupt Phase 3 with questions.

**Verdict Rules:**
- compliant: Document section explicitly addresses the requirement with specific, operational language
- partial: Some evidence exists but incomplete coverage (must include gap_description)
- not_found: No relevant evidence found in any document (NOT the same as non-compliant)
- contradicted: Document content actively contradicts the requirement
- not_applicable: Excluded via client attestation during intake

**Evidence Rules:**
- Every 'compliant' verdict MUST have at least one evidence_ref with doc_id, section_ref, page numbers
- Every 'partial' verdict MUST have both evidence_refs AND gap_description
- Verbatim quotes are required for high-confidence verdicts
- Search with at least 2 query variations before concluding 'not_found'

required_fields.message: Organization profile is required before proceeding
At least one document must be indexed
At least one framework must be selected
At least one requirement must be assessed
Scope and methodology section is required
Report must be marked as ready

rules.description: Every applicable requirement in the register must have a corresponding compliance matrix entry
rules.message_template: Requirement '{req_id}' has no compliance matrix entry

rules.description: Every 'compliant' verdict must have at least one evidence reference
rules.message_template: Requirement '{req_id}' is marked compliant but has no evidence references

rules.description: Every 'partial' verdict must include a gap description
rules.message_template: Requirement '{req_id}' is marked partial but has no gap description

rules.description: Every intake question must have a corresponding response
Every framework in enumeration metadata must have a recorded version
rules.message_template: Framework '{framework}' has no version recorded in enumeration_metadata

rules.description: High-confidence verdicts should include a verbatim quote from the source document
rules.message_template: Requirement '{req_id}' has high confidence but no verbatim quote

soft_warnings.message: Most frameworks have more than 10 requirements — verify enumeration is complete

evidence_summary.message: Matrix entry for '{req_id}' has a vague evidence summary. Specify what the document says.

phases.name: Scoping & Document Intake
phases.description: Index documents, build organization profile, identify frameworks, conduct intake interview

required_fields.message: At least one document must be indexed
At least one framework must be selected

rules.description: Every intake question must have a corresponding response

phases.name: Requirement Enumeration
phases.description: Enumerate all requirements from applicable frameworks, apply exclusions, form domain groups

required_fields.message: At least one requirement must be enumerated
Requirements must be grouped into domains

rules.description: Every framework in enumeration metadata must have a recorded version

soft_warnings.message: Most frameworks have more than 10 requirements — verify enumeration is complete

phases.name: Evidence Assessment
phases.description: Systematically assess every requirement against document evidence, building the compliance matrix

required_fields.message: At least one requirement must be assessed

rules.description: Every applicable requirement must have a matrix entry
rules.message_template: Requirement '{req_id}' has no compliance matrix entry

rules.description: Every compliant verdict must have evidence references
Every partial verdict must include a gap description
rules.message_template: Requirement '{req_id}' marked partial without gap description

rules.description: High-confidence verdicts should include a verbatim quote
rules.message_template: Requirement '{req_id}' has high confidence but no verbatim quote

evidence_summary.message: Matrix entry for '{req_id}' has a vague evidence summary

phases.name: Coverage Validation & Report Delivery
phases.description: Verify complete coverage, calculate statistics, produce scope and methodology, assemble and deliver the final compliance report document

required_fields.message: Scope and methodology section is required
Report must be marked as ready

sections: Executive Summary — compliance posture, key metrics
Scope & Methodology — frameworks assessed, documents analyzed, limitations
Compliance Matrix — full table with req_id, verdict, confidence, evidence, gaps
Gap Analysis — prioritized not_found, partial, and contradicted findings
Evidence Register — all document citations mapped to requirements
Client Attestations — intake responses that affected scoping
deliverable.instructions: Before calling wkfl_export_report, store these keys: org_profile, documents, applicable_frameworks, coverage_stats, scope_and_methodology. Domain groups from Phase 3 must already be stored with keys prefixed 'group_'. The tool assembles everything into a single Markdown report. Present the report to the user as the assessment deliverable.
